title: Proof Package Requirement for Blockchain Vendors, Grantees, and Partners
instructions: Attach this requirement to grants, RFPs, milestone agreements, partner approvals, and treasury vendor scopes.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: A single public place to inspect the claim and its evidence.
requiredFields.missingResult: The claim remains marketing, not diligence-ready.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: What was promised, by whom, under what acceptance criteria.
requiredFields.missingResult: No one can tell whether the work was actually complete.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: Artifacts, commits, reports, hashes, provenance, and boundaries.
requiredFields.missingResult: The project has claims, but no structured proof.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: How another reviewer can check the result again.
requiredFields.missingResult: The work cannot graduate from assertion to reviewable evidence.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: What independent or role-specific review passed, failed, or challenged.
requiredFields.missingResult: The claim should not become mature or settlement-ready.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: Known limitations, unresolved risks, rollback path, and challenge window.
requiredFields.missingResult: Stakeholders cannot price or govern the remaining risk.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: Who accepted, rejected, or held the claim.
requiredFields.missingResult: No release, payout, upgrade, or public reliance should occur.

requiredFields.whyItMatters: A durable decision record with hash commitments and boundary language.
requiredFields.missingResult: No serious credibility or settlement readiness.

acceptanceLanguage: Payment, recognition, launch support, listing support, public endorsement, or governance readiness should not proceed until the receipt-ready proof package is complete.
